timeline.text: Sample RF-104 collected in Clinic Room 2 by nurse practitioner PJ.
timeline.quote: Sample RF-104 was collected at 09:05 on 14 July 2026 in Clinic Room 2.

timeline.text: Sample placed in the transport rack outside the clinic room; no custody record from this point.
timeline.quote: I drew RF-104 first thing and put it in the transport rack outside room 2 as usual.

timeline.text: SOP-014 v3 escalation trigger reached (45 minutes from collection). No escalation recorded.
timeline.quote: Where a delay beyond 45 minutes from collection is expected, the receiving technician must escalate to the study coordinator by telephone
Whole-blood samples for the biomarker sub-study must begin processing within 60 minutes of collection.

timeline.text: Receipt time entered in the handoff log with no receiver identified.
Processing began, 95 minutes after collection, performed by technician LM.
timeline.quote: Processing began at 10:40 in the on-site processing laboratory.

timeline.text: Deviation identified by the quality function during routine sample-log review, not at the point of occurrence.
timeline.quote: The delay was discovered during end-of-day review of the sample log on 14 July 2026 by the quality function.

confirmed.text: Processing began 95 minutes after collection, exceeding the protocol's 60-minute limit by 35 minutes.
confirmed.quote: Processing began at 10:40 in the on-site processing laboratory.

confirmed.text: SOP-014 v3 was in force on the day of the event, having taken effect thirteen days earlier.
The 45-minute escalation trigger was introduced in v3 and did not exist in the version the processing technician was trained on.
confirmed.quote: Change note versus v2: the 45-minute escalation trigger in 4.2 is new in v3.

confirmed.text: No escalation to the study coordinator was recorded at any point.
Technician LM's training on the current SOP version was assigned but never completed.
confirmed.quote: SOP-014 v3 - Sample Receipt and Processing. Assigned 2 July 2026. Status: Assigned. No completion date recorded.

missing.text: The identity of the person who received the sample at 10:17 is not recorded, so custody cannot be established for the 72 minutes after collection.
No transport record exists between collection and receipt; the field was left empty rather than marked not applicable.
missing.quote: Transport to laboratory: field left blank.

missing.text: The collector states she does not know who transported the sample, so the gap cannot be closed from her account.
missing.quote: I do not know who took it across to the lab or when.

missing.text: Sample disposition has not been determined by the principal investigator as the protocol requires for out-of-window samples.
missing.quote: Reported impact: unknown at time of writing.

contradictions.text: The handoff log records a 10:17 receipt, but the quality function notes the entry appears to have been made after the fact, so the recorded time may not reflect actual receipt.
contradictions.quote: receipt entry appears to have been completed retrospectively; handwriting differs from the collection entry

contradictions.text: SOP-014 v3 requires the receiving technician to record receipt including initials, yet the log shows a receipt time with no receiver, meaning either the SOP was not followed or the entry was not made by a receiving technician.
contradictions.quote: The receiving technician must record receipt in the sample handoff log, including printed name or initials

hypotheses.statement: The sample sat unattended in the transport rack because no individual owned the transfer step between clinic and laboratory.
hypotheses.reasoning: The collector placed the sample in a rack and considered her task complete; the transport field is blank; no receiver is identified; 72 minutes are unaccounted for. The process appears to assume someone will collect the rack without naming who.
hypotheses.disconfirming_evidence: A courier log, rota, or CCTV showing a named person collected the sample promptly would rule this out.

hypotheses.statement: The escalation did not occur because the technician was operating under the superseded v2 rule, under which no escalation was due until the 60-minute limit had already passed.
hypotheses.reasoning: LM completed v2 training only; v3 training was assigned but not completed; the 45-minute trigger is new in v3. A technician following v2 would not have recognised 09:50 as an action point.
hypotheses.disconfirming_evidence: Evidence that LM was briefed on the v3 change outside formal training, or that LM did not have the sample before 10:17 and so could not have escalated.

hypotheses.statement: Change control released SOP v3 into effect without confirming that affected staff had completed training on it.
hypotheses.reasoning: v3 took effect 1 July; training was assigned 2 July, a day after the effective date, and remained incomplete thirteen days later while the technician continued to perform the activity.
hypotheses.disconfirming_evidence: A documented change-control gate showing training completion was verified before release, and that LM was excluded from the activity until trained.

hypotheses.statement: The deviation was detectable in real time but no control existed to surface it, so it was only found at end-of-day review.
hypotheses.reasoning: Both the 45-minute and 60-minute thresholds passed without any recorded action, and discovery came from a retrospective log review rather than from anyone involved on the day.
hypotheses.disconfirming_evidence: An alerting mechanism that did fire and was acknowledged, or evidence that staff raised the delay verbally without recording it.

capa.text: Obtain principal investigator determination of RF-104 sample disposition and document the scientific assessment of analyte stability.
capa.rationale: Protocol 7.2.3 requires PI determination for out-of-window samples; the deviation report records impact as unknown.

capa.text: Interview the collector, all staff with laboratory access on 14 July, and technician LM to reconstruct custody between 09:05 and 10:17; document who made the 10:17 entry and when.
capa.rationale: Addresses the unidentified receiver and the retrospective log entry.

capa.text: Complete and document SOP-014 v3 training for LM and all staff with the v3 assignment outstanding; restrict sample receipt to trained staff until complete.
capa.rationale: SOP-014 v3 section 6.0 requires current-version training before performing the activity.

capa.text: Add a named-owner transport step to the handoff log with a mandatory receiver signature, and reject log entries missing the receiver field.
capa.rationale: Addresses the ownership gap hypothesis at the point where custody was lost.

capa.text: Amend the change-control procedure so that an SOP version cannot take effect until training completion is confirmed for all staff performing the affected activity.
capa.rationale: Addresses the systemic pattern rather than this single training gap.

capa.text: Implement a 45-minute elapsed-time alert on collected samples awaiting processing, routed to the study coordinator.
capa.rationale: Makes the v3 escalation trigger detectable at the moment it is reached rather than at end-of-day review.
